SOFTWARE INPUTS (What Data Goes In) From Operations/Daily: - Train IDs and fleet type (Kinki Sharyo vs Alstom classification) - Daily mileage (live data per train) - Daily train fitness status - Daily operation requirements and run IDs - Daily deployment requirements across network From Maintenance/Planning: - Maintenance plans (PM schedules, overhauls, inspection intervals) - Track availability (how many maintenance tracks, capacity) - Man-hours per maintenance activity (fixed parameter) - Maintenance baseline costs (material + service per activity) - Man-hour labor costs - Base depot for each fleet (Al Rashidiya for Alstom, Jebel Ali for Kinki) --- 4. SOFTWARE OUTPUTS (What Dashboards/Reports Come Out) Dashboard Requirements: 1. Centralized Dashboard - High-level customized output with complete mileage status 2. Maintenance Status - Current status of all trains in terms of mileage 3. Train Run Assignments - Optimized daily allocation based on fitness & mileage balance 4. PM/Overhaul Activities - Predicted schedule of upcoming preventive maintenance 5. Compliance Status - Real-time tracking of adherence to maintenance requirements 6. Cost Compliance - Monthly/yearly comparison (actual vs planned) Reports/Exports: - Standard and custom report generation (PDF, Excel) - Simulation reports on different mileage management models - Email notifications to stakeholders --- 5. UI REQUIREMENTS (What They Specified) Critical Specifications: - Web-based application (preferred) - Desktop and mobile accessible - Single view paradigm - Not separate modules/tabs (implied by RFQ context) - Customizable alerts/notifications - Users need to tune warning thresholds - Full accessibility as admin - Admin has complete system access - Data security and compliance with IT standards - Scalable architecture - Support additional fleets and functionalities For Our Demo (Not Specified But Critical for Winning): - Intuitive semantic zoom (Google Earth style) to show multi-temporal planning - Visual conflict indicators (red highlighting for violations) - Real-time responsiveness (60fps target for 128 trains × 10 years data) - Professional UI polish (BlueprintJS enterprise component library) --- 6. PHASE DEPLOYMENT (What Phases Are Mentioned) Phase 1 (DEMO/MVP Scope) - Focus: Mileage management + run assignments visualization - Key Deliverables: - Mileage status tracking for all trains - Visualization of upcoming maintenance schedules - Visual indication of conflicts/overruns Phase 2 - Focus: Costing integration + output generation - Key Deliverables: - Maintenance cost forecasting per fleet - Report generation (PDF, Excel) - Simulation comparisons with cost impact Phase 3 - Focus: Additional fleets + competence management - Key Deliverables: - Support for more fleet types - User competence level management - Future scope TBD --- 7. BACKGROUND CONTEXT (Why They Need This) Business Driver: Dubai Metro operates 128 trains across two fleets: - Kinki Sharyo Fleet: 49 trains, newer, Green Line - Alstom Fleet: 79 trains, Red Line Maintenance Reality: - Each train needs A/B/C/D-level maintenance at different intervals (10km, 50km, 200km, 800km) - Maintenance takes time (A=4h, B=16h, C=72h, D=480h) - Each depot has limited tracks and man-hours (5,200/week combined) - Running trains generates mileage uncontrollably - can't just "pause" a fleet - Over-running a maintenance interval = safety/compliance violation - Under-running = wasted resources and cost Strategic Need: This is a 12-year contract. They need: 1. To know what's coming (predictive planning) 2. To balance workload (avoid depot bottlenecks) 3. To stay compliant (mileage tracking with warnings) 4. To model options ("what if we run this train here instead?") 5. To justify costs to stakeholders (per-fleet breakdown) --- 8. WHAT THE DEMO MUST DEMONSTRATE (To Win the Deal) To Impress the Client: | Demo Element | Why It Matters | What We Show | |---------------------|---------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------| | Semantic Zoom | Proves "Single Pane of Glass" concept | Pan 12 years, zoom to see daily details | | All 128 Trains | Shows system can handle scale | 50 trains in POC, clearly 128-capable | | 10-Year Timeline | Strategic planning capability | Full decade of task visualization | | Color-Coded Tasks | Maintenance vs Overhaul clarity | Blue blocks (maintenance), Orange (overhauls) | | Conflict Indicators | Compliance/risk management | Red highlighting on overlapping tasks | | Sidebar Train List | Operations-maintenance interface | Synced with vertical pan | | LOD Transitions | Technical sophistication | Heatmap → DNA blocks → Task cards | | Responsive 60fps | Professional quality | Smooth pan/zoom, no stuttering | What NOT To Show (Phase 2+): - Costing details (Phase 2) - Report generation (Phase 2) - Actual IBM Maximo integration (Phase 3) - Competence management (Phase 3) --- 9. FEATURE PRIORITY FOR CURRENT PHASE (What We're Building) Based on RFQ Phase 1 scope: MUST HAVE (MVP for Phase 1) 1. ✅ Data visualization - All 128 trains × 10 years of tasks 2. ✅ Semantic zoom - Heatmap → blocks → cards (LOD layers) 3. ✅ Maintenance schedule display - Show upcoming PM/overhauls 4. ✅ Conflict visualization - Red indicators for overlaps 5. ✅ Run assignment context - Which trains are scheduled when NICE TO HAVE (MVP Enhancement) 1. Task details on hover/click 2. Filter by fleet type (Kinki vs Alstom) 3. Search/find specific train 4. Time navigation (jump to date) PHASE 2+ (Don't Build Yet) 1. Actual cost calculations 2. Report generation 3. Simulation engine 4. Maximo integration --- 10. KEY SUCCESS METRICS FOR DEMO | Metric | Target | How to Verify | |-------------------------|---------------------------|--------------------------------------| | All trains visible | 50 trains (128-capable) | Count on screen | | Full time range | 10 years | Scroll entire timeline | | LOD transitions smooth | 60fps | No stuttering when zooming | | No console errors | Zero red errors | Browser DevTools | | Conflict detection | Visual indicators present | See red task blocks | | Professional appearance | Enterprise polish | BlueprintJS components, clean layout | --- Summary: Demo Success Criteria The client will be impressed if we show: 1. A 12-year timeline fully loaded with maintenance tasks 2. Smooth zoom from strategic heatmap (all capacity) to operational detail (individual task cards) 3. Two fleets (Kinki + Alstom) clearly distinguished 4. Conflict indicators showing maintenance gaps/overlaps 5. Responsive 60fps performance on 50-128 trains 6. Professional enterprise UI (BlueprintJS, consistent design) 7. Clear path to Phase 2 (costing, reporting, simulation) This is NOT yet: - A scheduling optimizer (Phase 2) - A cost analyzer (Phase 2) - An IBM Maximo integration (Phase 3) - A what-if simulator (Phase 2) It IS: - A visualization proof-of-concept that demonstrates the "Single Pane of Glass" concept - A visual audit tool to see maintenance status at any temporal scale - A risk detector showing conflicts and underrun/overrun warnings - A professional prototype that wins the contract to build Phase 2+ ⎿  Done (3 tool uses · 54.3k tokens · 43s) ⏺ Explore(Analyze DESIGN_SPEC issues and references) ⎿  Prompt: Read docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md and identify: 1.  10651→       MMS Project Structure Analysis - Complete Report 
 10652→ 
 10653→       Based on a thorough exploration of the Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS) codebase, here's a structured summary of what currently 
 10654→       exists: 
 10655→ 
 10656→       1. PROJECT OVERVIEW 
 10657→ 
 10658→       Current State: Production React + deck.gl application with extensive POC components and story-based development approach 
 10659→       Tech Stack: Vite 5.x + React 18.3.1 + deck.gl 9.2.5 + XState 5.x + Tailwind CSS 
 10660→       Entry Point: /apps/canvas/src/ 
 10661→       Status: Design revision phase - CRITICAL document update needed 
 10662→ 
 10663→       --- 
 10664→       2. STORYBOOK POCs (apps/canvas/src/stories/) 
 10665→ 
 10666→       01-POCs Directory (Proof of Concept Experiments) 
 10667→ 
 10668→       - Interaction/GestureRecognizer.stories.tsx - Mouse/touch gesture handling POC 
 10669→       - Rendering/DeckGLHeatmap.stories.tsx - deck.gl heatmap visualization POC 
 10670→       - Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx - Semantic zoom concept validation 
 10671→       - State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx - XState viewport state machine POC 
 10672→ 
 10673→       02-Layers Directory (Layer Components in Development) 
 10674→ 
 10675→       Components: 
 10676→       - GanttBar.tsx - Task bar visualization component 
 10677→       - KPICard.tsx - Key Performance Indicator cards 
 10678→       - TimelineHeader.tsx - Adaptive timeline header component 
 10679→       - TodayLine.tsx - Current date indicator 
 10680→ 
 10681→       Stories by Layer: 
 10682→       - Strategic/Strategic.stories.tsx + StrategicLayer.tsx - 12-year heatmap view 
 10683→       - Tactical/Tactical.stories.tsx + TacticalLayer.tsx - 2-5 year task strips view 
 10684→       - Operational/Operational.stories.tsx + OperationalLayer.tsx - Operational scheduling view 
 10685→       - Execution/Execution.stories.tsx + ExecutionLayer.tsx - Daily execution layer 
 10686→       - Components/Components.stories.tsx - UI component library showcase 
 10687→ 
 10688→       03-Demo Directory (Full Application Stories) 
 10689→ 
 10690→       - FractalCanvas.stories.tsx + FractalCanvas.tsx - Main interactive demo 
 10691→       - FractalCanvasComplete.stories.tsx + FractalCanvasComplete.tsx - Complete feature demo 
 10692→       - ConstraintDeck.tsx - Constraint adjustment UI component 
 10693→       - TimeScrubber.tsx - Timeline scrubber/time selector component 
 10694→ 
 10695→       time-scrubber Directory (Time Navigation POCs - 7 Variants) 
 10696→ 
 10697→       1. BlueprintRangeSlider.stories.tsx + BlueprintRangeSlider.tsx - Blueprint range slider variant 
 10698→       2. D3BrushTimeScrubber.stories.tsx + D3BrushTimeScrubber.tsx - D3 brush-based variant 
 10699→       3. DeckGLFilterDemo.stories.tsx + DeckGLFilterDemo.tsx - deck.gl data filter approach 
 10700→       4. LTTBDownsampleDemo.stories.tsx + LTTBDownsampleDemo.tsx - LTTB downsampling algorithm

 10974→       RFQ Analysis: Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS) Demo Requirements 
 10975→ 
 10976→       --- 
 10977→       1. CLIENT PAIN POINTS (Current State - What They're Suffering With) 
 10978→ 
 10979→       Critical Problems Today: 
 10980→       - No dedicated mileage tools - Managing everything manually via Excel 
 10981→       - Data chaos - Raw mileage stored in folders, requires validation, no system integration 
 10982→       - Compliance risk - Manual tracking causes mileage underrun/overrun issues with no safeguards 
 10983→       - Team disconnection - Maintenance and Operations teams can't coordinate on mileage decisions 
 10984→       - Zero visibility - Don't know if resources (man-hours, tracks) are over/under-utilized 
 10985→       - Slow decisions - Manual multi-source data processing causes delays and inefficiencies 
 10986→       - No simulation capability - Can't model "what-if" scenarios for planning 
 10987→ 
 10988→       Why This Matters: 
 10989→       - Fleet growing (increasing trains) 
 10990→       - Two different fleet types (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom) need different strategies 
 10991→       - 128 trains total - impossible to manage manually 
 10992→       - 12-year contract requires predictive planning, not firefighting 
 10993→

 10996→

       --- 
       MMS Project Structure Analysis - Complete Report 
 
       Based on a thorough exploration of the Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS) codebase, here's a structured summary of what currently 
       exists: 
 
       1. PROJECT OVERVIEW 
 
       Current State: Production React + deck.gl application with extensive POC components and story-based development approach 
       Tech Stack: Vite 5.x + React 18.3.1 + deck.gl 9.2.5 + XState 5.x + Tailwind CSS 
       Entry Point: /apps/canvas/src/ 
       Status: Design revision phase - CRITICAL document update needed 
 
       --- 
       2. STORYBOOK POCs (apps/canvas/src/stories/) 
 
       01-POCs Directory (Proof of Concept Experiments) 
 
       - Interaction/GestureRecognizer.stories.tsx - Mouse/touch gesture handling POC 
       - Rendering/DeckGLHeatmap.stories.tsx - deck.gl heatmap visualization POC 
       - Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx - Semantic zoom concept validation 
       - State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx - XState viewport state machine POC 
 
       02-Layers Directory (Layer Components in Development) 
 
       Components: 
       - GanttBar.tsx - Task bar visualization component 
       - KPICard.tsx - Key Performance Indicator cards 
       - TimelineHeader.tsx - Adaptive timeline header component 
       - TodayLine.tsx - Current date indicator 
 
       Stories by Layer: 
       - Strategic/Strategic.stories.tsx + StrategicLayer.tsx - 12-year heatmap view 
       - Tactical/Tactical.stories.tsx + TacticalLayer.tsx - 2-5 year task strips view 
       - Operational/Operational.stories.tsx + OperationalLayer.tsx - Operational scheduling view 
       - Execution/Execution.stories.tsx + ExecutionLayer.tsx - Daily execution layer 
       - Components/Components.stories.tsx - UI component library showcase 
 
       03-Demo Directory (Full Application Stories) 
 
       - FractalCanvas.stories.tsx + FractalCanvas.tsx - Main interactive demo 
       - FractalCanvasComplete.stories.tsx + FractalCanvasComplete.tsx - Complete feature demo 
       - ConstraintDeck.tsx - Constraint adjustment UI component 
       - TimeScrubber.tsx - Timeline scrubber/time selector component 
 
       time-scrubber Directory (Time Navigation POCs - 7 Variants) 
 
       1. BlueprintRangeSlider.stories.tsx + BlueprintRangeSlider.tsx - Blueprint range slider variant 
       2. D3BrushTimeScrubber.stories.tsx + D3BrushTimeScrubber.tsx - D3 brush-based variant 
       3. DeckGLFilterDemo.stories.tsx + DeckGLFilterDemo.tsx - deck.gl data filter approach 
       4. LTTBDownsampleDemo.stories.tsx + LTTBDownsampleDemo.tsx - LTTB downsampling algorithm 
       5. RechartsTimeScrubber.stories.tsx + RechartsTimeScrubber.tsx - Recharts library variant 
       6. UplotTimeScrubber.stories.tsx + UplotTimeScrubber.tsx - uPlot library variant 
       7. VisxTimeScrubber.stories.tsx + VisxTimeScrubber.tsx - Visx library variant 
       - lttbAlgorithm.ts - Largest Triangle Three Buckets downsampling implementation 
       - mockData.ts - Mock data generator for time scrubber tests 
 
       data Directory 
 
       - index.ts - Re-exports 
       - mock-config.ts - Configuration for mock data 
       - mock-generator.ts - Data generation utilities 
 
       machines Directory 
 
       - index.ts - Re-exports 
       - viewport.machine.ts - XState viewport state machine for zoom/pan 
 
       --- 
       3. MAIN APPLICATION COMPONENTS (apps/canvas/src/) 
 
       Core Files 
 
       - main.tsx - React entry point 
       - App.tsx - Main application component (13,459 bytes) - manages zoom, LOD, sidebar, tooltips, minimap 
       - index.css - Global styles 
 
       components/ Directory (Blueprint.js UI Components) 
 
       - TimelineHeader.tsx (9,587 bytes) - Adaptive timeline with date labels (handles macro/meso/micro granularity) 
       - TaskTooltip.tsx (6,686 bytes) - Task detail popup on hover 
       - TaskDetailsPanel.tsx (12,365 bytes) - Detailed task information sidebar 
       - Minimap.tsx (11,185 bytes) - Overview navigator synced with canvas 
       - PerfMonitor.tsx (2,263 bytes) - Performance metrics display toggle 
       - StatsMonitor.tsx (7,300 bytes) - Statistics and performance panel 
 
       deck/ Directory (Visualization Engine - deck.gl) 
 
       Main Canvas: 
       - DeckCanvas.tsx (21,620 bytes) - Main deck.gl canvas orchestrator 
 
       Layers/: 
       - MacroLayer.ts (9,464 bytes) - Strategic heatmap layer (zoom < 0.5x) 
       - MesoLayer.ts (10,839 bytes) - Tactical task strips layer (0.5x - 2x zoom) 
       - MicroLayer.ts (17,547 bytes) - Operational detailed cards layer (zoom > 2x) 
       - index.ts - Layer exports 
 
       Utilities: 
       - hooks/useCanvasGestures.ts - Pan/zoom gesture handling 
       - spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts - R-tree spatial indexing for O(log n) queries 
       - spatial/index.ts - Spatial utilities 
 
       data/ Directory (Domain Data) 
 
       - types.ts (320 lines) - Comprehensive type definitions: 
         - Domain types: Train, Task, DatasetMeta, GeneratedData 
         - Rendering types: TaskRender, TaskColor 
         - Configuration: ViewConfig, ViewportState, WorldBounds, DataBounds 
         - Utility functions: calculateWorldBounds(), calculateDataBounds(), calculateFitToView() 
       - generator.ts - Mock data generator (50 trains, 10 years, ~60k tasks) 
 
       state/ Directory (XState State Management) 
 
       - canvas-machine.ts (2,576 bytes) - Main XState machine definition 
       - viewport.ts (6,413 bytes) - Viewport/camera slice (zoom, pan, LOD, animation) 
       - interaction.ts (3,651 bytes) - Interaction slice (hover, selection, mouse position) 
       - index.ts (1,321 bytes) - Re-exports and useCanvasSelector hook 
 
       entity-store/ Directory (Alternative Entity Store) 
 
       - EntityStore.ts - Typed entity storage system 
       - types.ts - Entity type definitions 
       - index.ts - Re-exports

       RFQ Analysis: Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS) Demo Requirements 
 
       --- 
       1. CLIENT PAIN POINTS (Current State - What They're Suffering With) 
 
       Critical Problems Today: 
       - No dedicated mileage tools - Managing everything manually via Excel 
       - Data chaos - Raw mileage stored in folders, requires validation, no system integration 
       - Compliance risk - Manual tracking causes mileage underrun/overrun issues with no safeguards 
       - Team disconnection - Maintenance and Operations teams can't coordinate on mileage decisions 
       - Zero visibility - Don't know if resources (man-hours, tracks) are over/under-utilized 
       - Slow decisions - Manual multi-source data processing causes delays and inefficiencies 
       - No simulation capability - Can't model "what-if" scenarios for planning 
 
       Why This Matters: 
       - Fleet growing (increasing trains) 
       - Two different fleet types (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom) need different strategies 
       - 128 trains total - impossible to manage manually

     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 
     │ WHAT IS A HARNESS?                                          │ 
     ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ 
     │ A 'Harness' is a framework that enables AI agents to work   │ 
     │ on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.     │ 
     │                                                              │ 
     │ PROBLEM: Each new session starts with NO memory.            │ 
     │ SOLUTION: State files (progress + features) persist context.│ 
     │                                                              │ 
     │ Official source: anthropic.com/engineering/                 │ 
     │                  effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents│ 
     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 
 
     📅 Current date: 2025-12-20 
 
     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 
     │ WORK PROTOCOL                                               │ 
     ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ 
     │ 1. READ claude-progress.txt → session history              │ 
     │ 2. READ features.json → next feature (passes:false)        │ 
     │ 3. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time                          │ 
     │ 4. TEST visually with Playwright MCP                        │ 
     │ 5. RUN QA PROTOCOL (see docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md)               │ 
     │ 6. COMMIT after feature passes                              │ 
     │ 7. UPDATE claude-progress.txt                               │ 
     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ 
 
     ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ 
     │ 🔄 SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL                                 │ 
     ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ 
     │ If you DON'T KNOW what a 'Harness' is:                      │ 
     │                                                              │ 
     │ 1. SEARCH: WebSearch 'Anthropic harness agents 2025'        │ 
     │ 2. ANALYZE: Why was local documentation unclear?            │ 
     │ 3. FIX: Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity        │ 
     │ 4. DOCUMENT: Record the fix in claude-progress.txt          │ 
     │                                                              │ 
     │ This is a CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT system.                    │ 
     │ Each session should leave harness clearer for the next.     │ 
     └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

content.thinking:
Excelente! Agora tenho informações cruciais. Vou resumir os erros que encontrei:

## ERROS CRÍTICOS NO PLANO FINAL (linhas 11844-12136):

### ERRO 1: Confusão sobre Stack de Renderização
No plano (linha 11903-11908), diz:
```
Stack Correto:
Visualization: deck.gl 9.2.5 com OrthographicView
```
Mas anteriormente na análise (linha 11522-11527), o Claude escreveu:
```
**⚠️ MAJOR ISSUE:** Document says "deck.gl" but project uses R3F (React Three Fiber) + Three.js for rendering, not deck.gl!
```
Isso está **ERRADO**! O projeto **JÁ MIGROU** de R3F para deck.gl, conforme visto nos commits:
- `a4fa495 feat(deck): complete R3F removal and TypeScript strict mode restoration`
- `7ef9708 feat(deck): DECK-MIGRATE-002 Phase 2 - Layer Migration to deck.gl`
- `eed2f65 feat(deck): DECK-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1 - deck.gl foundation`

O features.json também confirma (linhas 656-663):
```
"from": "React Three Fiber (R3F) + Three.js",
"to": "deck.gl with OrthographicView"
"COMPLETE_MIGRATION_REQUIRED"
```

E mostra que a migração está **COMPLETA** ("passes": true).

### ERRO 2: Arquivar código para apps/canvas-old/
O plano (linha 11977-1990) sugere:
```
FASE 1: Arquivar e Criar Projeto
1. mv apps/canvas apps/canvas-old


2. Criar novo projeto Vite + React 18 em apps/canvas
```

Identifico uma abordagem equivocada. A proposta de mover o código atual é completamente desnecessária. O repositório `apps/canvas/` já possui:
- Tecnologias atualizadas
- Implementações completas das layers
- Estrutura funcional com POCs

A única pendência real é corrigir referências no **DESIGN_SPEC.md**.

Nas menções de Astro.js, o usuário comete um erro técnico. O projeto utiliza Vite + React 18, não Astro.js, e essa inconsistência precisa ser destacada.

O plano de copiar arquivos de `apps/canvas-old/` também é incorreto, pois os códigos já estão presentes no local correto.

Os caminhos dos POCs estão imprecisos, com referências que não correspondem à estrutura real do projeto. A localização correta dos arquivos deve ser `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/...`.

A análise apresenta contradições factuais, especialmente na seção sobre tecnologias do projeto, indicando necessidade de revisão cuidadosa dos detalhes técnicos.

O plano proposto envolve uma abordagem destrutiva e desnecessária, sugerindo a movimentação de código funcional para um diretório antigo e posterior recriação, o que parece ineficiente e potencialmente arriscado.

A documentação técnica mistura referências a diferentes frameworks - R3F, Three.js e deck.gl - criando confusão sobre a stack tecnológica real do projeto, que já migrou completamente para deck.gl.

A solução pragmática requer ajustes mínimos: manter o código existente, corrigir a documentação no DESIGN_SPEC.md e focar em refinamentos e documentação, sem reconstruir completamente a aplicação.

content.text:
---

# 🔴 ANÁLISE DE ERROS NO PLANO FINAL

Após analisar o chat do fim para o começo, encontrei **VÁRIOS ERROS CRÍTICOS** no plano criado (linhas 11844-12136).

---

## ERRO 1: CONTRADIÇÃO GRAVE - Stack de Renderização

### O que a análise diz (ERRADO):
> **Linha 11522**: "⚠️ MAJOR ISSUE: Document says 'deck.gl' but project uses R3F (React Three Fiber) + Three.js for rendering, not deck.gl!"

### A realidade (CORRETO):
O projeto **JÁ MIGROU** de R3F para deck.gl. Os commits mostram claramente:

```
a4fa495 feat(deck): complete R3F removal and TypeScript strict mode restoration
7ef9708 feat(deck): DECK-MIGRATE-002 Phase 2 - Layer Migration to deck.gl
eed2f65 feat(deck): DECK-MIGRATE-001 Phase 1 - deck.gl foundation
```

O `features.json` confirma:
```json
"DECK-CLEANUP-001": {
  "passes": true,
  "notes": "COMPLETE: Removed all R3F/Three.js imports... App renders with deck.gl only at 120fps."
}
```

**O Claude analisou errado: achou que R3F era atual, mas a migração para deck.gl está COMPLETA.**

---

## ERRO 2: PLANO DESTRUTIVO - Mover código funcional

### O que o plano propõe (ERRADO):
> **Linha 11977-1990**: "mv apps/canvas apps/canvas-old", "Criar novo projeto Vite + React 18 em apps/canvas"

### Por que está errado:
O código em `apps/canvas/` **JÁ FUNCIONA** com:
- ✅ deck.gl 9.2.5
- ✅ React 18.3.1 
- ✅ BlueprintJS 6.1.0
- ✅ XState implementado
- ✅ Todas as 3 layers (Macro, Meso, Micro)
- ✅ 45+ stories POCs
- ✅ Componentes UI (Minimap, Timeline, Tooltip)

**Mover para `canvas-old/` e recriar do zero é DESNECESSÁRIO e DESTRUTIVO.**

---

## ERRO 3: CONFUSÃO Astro.js vs Vite

### O prompt do usuário (ERRADO):
> **Linha 402-403**: "a implementação do frontend interativo com Astro.js e deck.gl"

### A realidade:
O projeto usa **Vite + React 18**, não Astro.js. O Claude não corrigiu o usuário sobre isso.

O `package.json` mostra:
```json
"@deck.gl/react": "^9.2.5",
"react": "18.3.1",
"vite": "5.x"
```

**Não há Astro.js no projeto.**

---

## ERRO 4: Caminhos de arquivo incorretos

### O plano diz (ERRADO):
> **Linha 11891**: "stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx"

### O correto:
```
apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/State/XStateViewport.stories.tsx
```

Os caminhos no plano omitem `apps/canvas/src/`.

---

## ERRO 5: O plano propõe copiar de canvas-old/

### O que o plano diz (ERRADO):
> **Linha 12003-2009**: 
> "Copiar de apps/canvas-old/:
> - stories/machines/viewport.machine.ts → src/state/viewport.machine.ts"

### Por que está errado:
1. **apps/canvas-old/ não existe** - seria criado pelo próprio plano
2. O código **JÁ ESTÁ** em `apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts`
3. O plano propõe destruir código funcional para recriá-lo

---

## ERRO 6: Análise auto-contraditória

A análise diz duas coisas contraditórias:

| Seção | O que diz |
|-------|-----------|
| Linha 11522 | "project uses R3F, not deck.gl!" (ERRADO) |
| Linha 11903 | "Stack: deck.gl 9.2.5" (CORRETO) |

**O Claude se contradisse dentro do mesmo plano.**

---

## 🟢 O QUE REALMENTE PRECISA SER FEITO

| Problema Real | Solução |
|---------------|---------|
| DESIGN_SPEC.md menciona "Astro.js" | Corrigir para "Vite + React 18" |
| Imagens de referência irrelevantes | Baixar novas (Epicflow, Dime Scheduler) |
| Sandbox Mode sub-especificado | Adicionar state machine, persistence |
| Código funcional | **NÃO TOCAR** - está funcionando |

---

## 📋 RESUMO DOS ERROS

| # | Tipo | Descrição |
|---|------|-----------|
| 1 | Factual | Claude achou que projeto usa R3F, mas já migrou para deck.gl |
| 2 | Destrutivo | Plano propõe mover código funcional e recriar do zero |
| 3 | Omissão | Não corrigiu usuário sobre Astro.js vs Vite |
| 4 | Técnico | Caminhos de arquivo incorretos |
| 5 | Lógico | Propõe copiar de diretório que não existe |
| 6 | Consistência | Plano se contradiz (R3F vs deck.gl) |

---

## RECOMENDAÇÃO

**NÃO execute o plano como está.** O código funciona. O problema é apenas:
1. Documentação (DESIGN_SPEC.md) precisa correção
2. Imagens de referência precisam atualização
3. Sandbox Mode precisa especificação detalhada

**O projeto NÃO precisa de reimplementação do zero.**

Aguardo suas instruções.

content.text:
## 🟢 O APP FUNCIONA!

Olhe o screenshot: **120 FPS**, tasks renderizando, sidebar, timeline, minimap, tudo funcionando!

O erro `maxTextureDimension2D` no console é um warning do WebGL headless, não impede nada.

---

## A VERDADE SOBRE O PROJETO

| O que você achava | A realidade |
|-------------------|-------------|
| "Projeto confuso e bugado" | App funciona a 120fps |
| "Não tenho algo funcional" | Screenshot mostra app completo |
| "Cheio de coisa antiga" | R3F foi **removido** - só deck.gl existe |
| "Precisa recriar do zero" | **NÃO** - seria desperdício |

---

## O QUE REALMENTE ESTÁ "CONFUSO"

1. **Documentação errada** - DESIGN_SPEC.md menciona Astro.js e outras coisas incorretas
2. **Muitos arquivos** - Há POCs em `stories/` + código de produção em `src/` - mas ambos funcionam
3. **O plano anterior** - Propôs destruir código funcional baseado em análise errada

---

## O QUE VOCÊ DEVE FAZER

### ❌ NÃO FAZER:
- Mover código para `canvas-old/`
- Recriar projeto do zero
- Perder 3+ dias refazendo o que funciona

### ✅ FAZER:

| Tarefa | Esforço | Impacto |
|--------|---------|---------|
| 1. Corrigir DESIGN_SPEC.md (Astro→Vite) | 30min | Alto |
| 2. Corrigir warning WebGL | 15min | Baixo |
| 3. Implementar Sandbox Mode | 1-2 dias | **CRÍTICO** |
| 4. Polish visual (tema dark, tipografia) | 1 dia | Alto |
| 5. Baixar imagens de referência corretas | 1h | Médio |

---

## PRÓXIMO PASSO IMEDIATO

Quer que eu:

1. **Corrija o DESIGN_SPEC.md** - Remover referências a Astro.js, atualizar stack
2. **Investigue o warning WebGL** - Provavelmente 5 linhas de código
3. **Comece o Sandbox Mode** - A feature que falta para a demo

O app **funciona**. O problema é documentação, não código.

message.content:
Veja a analise sensata que fez o Gemini: Com base na leitura detalhada do log completo (do fim para o começo e analisando o contexto histórico), identifiquei **5 erros críticos** e várias inconsistências no "Plano de Reimplementação" final proposto pelo agente.

O agente focou muito em corrigir a "alucinação do Astro.js", mas ao criar o plano "do zero", ele descartou decisões de arquitetura cruciais que já haviam sido validadas e solicitadas.

Aqui estão os erros encontrados no plano:

### 1. Quebra da Arquitetura de Monorepo (`packages/toolkit`)

* **O Erro:** Na **Fase 1** e **Fase 2** do plano, o agente sugere instalar `xstate` diretamente em `apps/canvas` e criar arquivos de máquina de estado locais (`src/state/`).
* **O Problema:** O log mostra claramente (Source 3787, 3766) que o projeto é um monorepo e já possui um pacote compartilhado **`packages/toolkit`** contendo lógicas de `state-machine`, `schema` e `helpers`.
* **Consequência:** Implementar o estado localmente ignora o código compartilhado já existente e duplica lógica, violando a estrutura do projeto que o usuário tentava preservar.

### 2. Ignorou o Requisito Específico do "Streetscape.gl"

* **O Erro:** O usuário solicitou explicitamente (Source 2172, 3343): *"Precisamos de algo que visualmente também inclua recurso igual ao streetscape.gl... podemos inclusive baixa-lo e reaproveitar parte do codigo"*.
* **O Problema:** O plano final menciona apenas `deck.gl` padrão. Não há menção na **Fase 3 (Semantic Zoom)** ou **Fase 4 (Visual Polish)** sobre a análise ou importação de componentes/estilos do `streetscape.gl` (como o "Time Scrubber" ou controles de playback que existem nessa lib).
* **Consequência:** O resultado final não atenderia à expectativa visual específica do cliente mencionada no prompt.

### 3. Priorização Incorreta do RBush (Performance Crítica)

* **O Erro:** O plano coloca a implementação do **RBush spatial index** apenas na **Fase 6: Polish Final**.
* **O Problema:** O contexto (Source 2135) confirma que o RBush é necessário para lidar com 60k+ tarefas e garantir *hover* performático.
* **Consequência:** Se o RBush for deixado para o final, a **Fase 3 (Semantic Zoom)** e a **Fase 5 (Sandbox Mode)** serão impossíveis de testar ou validar corretamente (ficarão lentas/travando) devido à quantidade de dados, fazendo com que o "Must Work Perfectly" falhe antes da fase final. O RBush é estrutural, não "polimento".

### 4. Dependências "Ocultas" (D3 Modules)

* **O Erro:** Na **Fase 4 (Visual Polish)**, o plano lista criar `TimelineHeader` e `Minimap`.
* **O Problema:** O histórico (Source 2146, 2150) mostra que foi feito um trabalho extenso (`D3-MIGRATE-001`) para integrar módulos específicos (`d3-scale`, `d3-time`, `d3-array`) para que esses componentes funcionassem corretamente com as datas e escalas.
* **Consequência:** O plano de instalação da **Fase 1** esqueceu de incluir essas bibliotecas D3. Sem elas, a recriação desses componentes falhará ou terá que ser reinventada do zero com lógica inferior.

### 5. Priorização do "Sandbox Mode" (Feature Principal)

* **O Erro:** O plano coloca o **Sandbox Mode** na **Fase 5**, quase no fim do projeto.
* **O Problema:** O prompt do usuário e a RFQ definem o Sandbox/What-if como a **"Primary Feature"** e a dor principal do cliente.
* **Consequência:** Deixar a funcionalidade mais complexa e importante para o final aumenta o risco. A arquitetura de estado (Fase 2) precisa ser desenhada *ao redor* do Sandbox (suporte a diffs, undo/redo, forks de estado) desde o dia 1, não "acoplada" depois que a visualização estática estiver pronta.

### Resumo das Correções Necessárias no Plano:

1. **Phase 1:** Adicionar `d3-*` packages e manter estrutura de monorepo (linkar `packages/toolkit` em vez de instalar xstate local).
2. **Phase 2:** Arquitetar o `EntityStore` (Source 1980) e o Estado considerando o Sandbox desde o início.
3. **Phase 3:** Integrar **RBush** imediatamente junto com as Layers do deck.gl.
4. **Geral:** Adicionar passo explícito para analisar/portar componentes do `streetscape.gl` conforme solicitado.

content.thinking:
O Gemini fez uma análise muito mais profunda e sensata. Ele identificou problemas arquiteturais e de priorização que eu não peguei porque foquei apenas na questão "o app funciona ou não".

Deixe-me analisar cada ponto do Gemini:

### 1. Quebra da Arquitetura de Monorepo (`packages/toolkit`)
- O Gemini está certo. O projeto tem `packages/toolkit` com state-machine compartilhado
- O plano propõe criar estado local em `apps/canvas/src/state/` duplicando lógica
- Isso viola a arquitetura do monorepo

### 2. Ignorou o Requisito Específico do "Streetscape.gl"
- Preciso verificar se isso foi realmente pedido no chat
- Se foi, é um erro grave ignorar

### 3. Priorização Incorreta do RBush (Performance Crítica)
- RBush é necessário para 60k+ tarefas
- Colocar na Fase 6 (Polish) é erro - deveria ser estrutural na Fase 3
- Sem RBush, hover em 60k tarefas será lento

### 4. Dependências "Ocultas" (D3 Modules)
- O histórico mostra D3-MIGRATE-001 foi feito
- d3-scale, d3-time, d3-array são necessários para TimelineHeader e Minimap
- Fase 1 não lista essas dependências

### 5. Priorização do "Sandbox Mode" (Feature Principal)
- Sandbox é a feature principal para a demo
- Colocar na Fase 5 (quase no fim) é arriscado
- O estado deveria ser arquitetado considerando Sandbox desde o início

O Gemini está correto em todos os pontos. Minha análise foi superficial - eu apenas verifiquei se o app atual roda, mas não analisei a qualidade do plano de reimplementação proposto.
 
Preciso investigar a real necessidade de recriar o aplicativo. O desempenho atual de 120fps sugere que a reimplementação pode ser desnecessária. Vou avaliar criticamente os pontos levantados, verificando se representam problemas reais ou apenas possíveis otimizações marginais.

A abordagem será:
1. Validar os problemas arquiteturais apontados
2. Comparar o custo de refatoração versus benefícios potenciais
3. Manter o foco na funcionalidade e performance atual

Verificarei detalhadamente o estado do projeto para determinar se a recriação trará ganhos significativos ou apenas complexidade adicional.

## Session: 2025-12-20 (Harness Analysis & Recovery)

### Task: Analyze context loss and improve harness protocol

**Objective:** Analyze last 2 commits and Claude logs to understand what was done correctly, hallucinations, and context loss.

### Findings

#### 1. Context Loss Analysis

| Metric | Before | After | Lost |
|--------|--------|-------|------|
| claude-progress.txt | 2338 lines | 205 lines | **91%** |
| features.json | 392 lines | 142 lines | **64%** |

**Root Cause:** Sessions saved to harness only at the end, not incrementally.

#### 2. Hallucinations in DESIGN_SPEC.md

| Tech Stack | Vite + React 18 | "Astro.js 4.x" |

#### 3. What Was Done Correctly

#### 4. POCs That Exist (from backup)

| deck.gl Heatmap | `stories/01-POCs/Rendering/` | ✅ |
| FractalCanvas | `stories/FractalCanvas*.tsx` | ✅ |

### New Harness Rules Added

1. Save BEFORE starting any task
2. Save AFTER each subtask
3. Checkpoint every 15-20 minutes
4. Save before intensive operations
5. If context > 70%, STOP and save

### Anthropic Harness Research (Completed)

Source: [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

**Key Learnings Applied:**
- JSON preferred for state files (less likely to be overwritten)
- Failure pattern 1: Doing too much → context exhaustion
- Failure pattern 2: Declaring done prematurely
- Incremental commits enable recovery
- Session startup rituals: always read context before acting

### Next Steps

1. Continue with DESIGN-REVISION task
2. Fetch new reference screenshots
3. Correct tech stack in DESIGN_SPEC.md

**Status:** Harness protocol improved ✅

## Session: 2025-12-19 (Critical Analysis)

### Executive Summary

The DESIGN_SPEC.md document has **CRITICAL issues** that must be resolved before implementation. A thorough analysis was completed and new reference sources were identified by the user.

---

## 🔴 CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND

### Issue 1: Wrong Reference Images

The downloaded reference images in `design/references/` have LOW implementation value:

| Image | Rating | Problem |
|-------|--------|---------|
| deck.gl/kepler-gl.jpg | 1/5 | Geospatial map - NOT timeline |
| deck.gl/flowmap.jpg | 1/5 | Flow visualization - NOT Gantt |
| deck.gl/sanddance.jpg | 1/5 | 3D statistical viz - irrelevant |
| linear/screenshots | 1-3/5 | Marketing pages, not UI patterns |
| railnova/railfleet.png | 2/5 | Splash page, not actual software |

The existing `design/screen-*.png` files (AI-generated) serve as inspiration only, not specification.

### Issue 2: Technology Stack Mismatch

Document says "Astro.js 4.x" but project uses Vite + React 18.

**Correct Stack:**
- Vite 5.x (NOT Astro)
- React 18.3.1
- deck.gl 9.2.5
- XState 5.x
- Tailwind CSS

### Issue 3: Sandbox Mode Underspecified

Sandbox Mode is the PRIMARY feature (addresses RFQ pain point #7 "No predictive simulations") but specification is vague. Missing:
- State machine diagram
- Persistence strategy
- Comparison algorithm
- Undo/redo implementation
- Conflict detection rules

### Issue 4: RFQ Pain Points Not Visually Addressed

Many solutions are abstract without concrete specifications.

### Issue 5: File Structure Mismatch

Document assumes Astro structure but project uses `apps/canvas/src/`.

---

## 🎯 NEW REFERENCE SOURCES (Critical - From User)

### Primary References (MUST Study)

| Source | URL | Purpose |
|--------|-----|---------|
| **Epicflow What-If** | https://www.epicflow.com/features/what-if/ | Sandbox Mode UI pattern |
| **Epicflow Pipeline** | https://www.epicflow.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/pipeline-implement.png | Visual planning layout |
| **Dime Scheduler** | https://www.dimescheduler.com/ | EXCELLENT - watch video! |
| **AVS Auto Demo** | https://avs.auto/demo/index.html | Single pane of glass example |
| **DHTMLX Gantt D3** | https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html | Gantt with deck.gl-like rendering |
| **Prometheus Group** | https://www.prometheusgroup.com/resources/posts/what-does-flipping-burgers-have-to-do-with-managing-asset-or-location-availability | Asset availability patterns |
| **IBM Maximo Scheduler** | Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler" | Industry standard reference |

### Design Philosophy Sources

| Source | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Linear App** | Modern aesthetic, dark mode, typography |
| **JetBrains Fleet (Islands UI)** | Enterprise layout density with clarity |
| **Google Maps** | Navigation fluidity (zoom/drag paradigm) |

### Screenshots to Capture (Next Session)

```
1. https://www.epicflow.com/features/what-if/ (what-if toggle, delta panel)
2. https://www.epicflow.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/pipeline-implement.png

4. https://avs.auto/demo/index.html (single pane example)
5. https://dhtmlx.github.io/demo-gantt-d3/index_1.html
6. Google Image Search: "IBM Maximo Maintenance Scheduler"
```

---

## User Decisions (Confirmed 2025-12-19)

1. **Tech Stack**: Vite + React 18 (correct from Astro.js)
2. **Sandbox Mode**: Detailed specification required (will be implemented later)
3. **Scope**: Planning View + Sandbox Mode focused, with roadmap for post-demo features
4. **References**: Use new high-quality sources, screen-*.png as inspiration only
5. **Design Goal**: Modern like Linear, but solid/corporate (not amateur). JetBrains Islands UI is good example.

---

## Revised Document Structure

### Part 1: Vision & RFQ Mapping
- Pain points → visual solutions → screen reference
- Demo focus: Planning View + Sandbox Mode
- Post-demo roadmap

### Part 2: Moodboard (REWRITE NEEDED)
**Primary Sources:**
